question:
Read the passage and answer the question.

'He saw more than ten ships sail past him, but none of them seemed to have a clue that he was there. After a few weeks, he thought he had at last got his lucky break when he managed to talk to the captain of an Indonesian ship by walkie-talkie.'

What does 'got his lucky break' mean?
choices: He finally had a fortunate opportunity
He broke a piece of equipment by accident
He managed to swim to safety
He found some supplies on the boat

question: Fill in the blank: A We ________ our options; we could try to open that window or break it.
Choose the correct binomial: After a hazardous journey, they arrived home ________.
Fill in the blank: She ________ the race if she trains hard. (first conditional)

Read the text and answer the question.

'Anyone who takes part in a competitive sport knows how the natural human desire to win can bring out the worst in people. Survival of the fittest is the name of the game, and can turn perfectly normal people into unpleasant individuals.'

What does 'bring out the worst in people' mean?
choices: Reveal people's most negative traits and behaviour
Force people to play sport against their will
Show how talented athletes can be
Encourage people to work together as a team

question: Choose the correct word: He was spending money in a very ________ way, buying things he didn't need.
Fill in the blank: I wish Ruba ________ out an hour ago. (I am unhappy she went out)
Choose the correct option: 'I should have asked if the boy needed any help.' This means it ________ a good idea to ask, so the speaker ________.
Fill in the blank: If I'd invested a few weeks in finding out about my business idea, it would have really ________.
Choose the correct third conditional: If they had written their homework, they ________ high marks.
Fill in the blank: He probably ________ bother; there were just a couple of old documents inside the safe.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'Safely back home with his family, Aldi considered his options. His family lived in some hardship and his job was, from his perspective, relatively lucrative at $134 a month.'

What is suggested about Aldi's job in this paragraph?
choices: The job was well-paid and relatively easy work
He risked his life by taking the job because he was poor
His parents were relieved when he gave up the job
He found a much better job after returning home

question: Choose the correct binomial: You really need to wear a helmet when you're on your bike. ________ you're going to injure yourself.
Fill in the blank: If I ________ Spanish, I'd never have met my Spanish friend. (mixed conditional)
Choose the correct option: 'I needn't have worried because most people aren't judging anyone else.' The word 'needn't' here means ________.
choices: It wasn't a good idea, but I did it
I did something, but it wasn't necessary
Something was possible, but I didn't do it
I didn't do something because it wasn't necessary

question: Fill in the blank: He is alone. If only I ________ with him. (present regret — I'm not with him now)
Choose the correct phrase: A potential ________ of the project is that it may take longer than expected.
Fill in the blank: The £6 million was a lot more ________ than the organisers thought it would be.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'Working as a teacher would appeal to me enormously for several reasons. I would very much value the opportunity to give something back to the people of Cambodia, rather than simply visiting as a tourist.'

What does the writer mean by 'give something back'?
choices: To return money she borrowed from the people
To contribute positively to the local community
To teach the local people about her own culture
To visit Cambodia as a tourist and take photos

question: Choose the correct mixed conditional: If Issa ________ afraid of snakes, he would have picked it up.
Fill in the blank: I wish I ________ here to watch me perform yesterday. (past regret)
Choose the correct option: 'Peace and quiet' means ________.
Fill in the blank: As part of my ________, I was responsible for organising events and managing volunteers.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'I have studied English to a high level and believe that my skills in this language would enable me to teach it successfully. Furthermore, I do have some experience of teaching English already on a children's summer camp.'

What phrase does the writer use to add more information?
Choose the correct form: If only I ________ to school yesterday instead of going by bike. (past regret — I should have walked)
Fill in the blank: There's a ________ chance that you might succeed, so be prepared for rejection.
Choose the correct option: 'Although some people might disagree, I can't help feeling that …' This phrase is used to ________.
choices: introduce the topic of the discussion
present only the negative aspects of something
give a personal opinion while acknowledging other views may exist
write the opening paragraph of a letter

question: Fill in the blank: If only he ________ earlier! (past regret — he didn't visit when he should have)
Choose the correct conditional: 'If video games weren't so costly, I'd buy a new one every month.' This is a ________.
Fill in the blank: I wish my sister ________ borrowing my clothes! (complaint about someone's ongoing behaviour)

Read the passage and answer the question.

'If only I had followed my dream to be a photographer! I was too scared of failure. I might not have been good enough, but I should at least have tried.'

What does the writer regret in this passage?
choices: Spending too much money on photography equipment
Not pursuing their dream of becoming a photographer

question: Choose the correct word: So when I saw him, I decided to try and ________ amends for what I had done wrong.
Fill in the blank: I am fully ________ to teach English at secondary level.
Choose the correct option: 'If you don't buy the car today, it will be more expensive tomorrow.' This means you are ________ to buy the car today.
Fill in the blank: If she ________ interesting, the journalists wouldn't have interviewed her. (mixed conditional: present fact affecting past)
Choose the correct option: 'I gained a qualification in …' is a phrase used in a letter of application to ________.

Read the passage and answer the question.

'He caught fish and used parts of his wooden hut to make a fire and cook the fish. Not having any more fresh drinking water, he had no choice but to drink sea water. However, he filtered the sea water through his clothes to reduce the amount of salt.'

What technique did Aldi use to make the sea water safer to drink?
choices: He boiled it over a fire
He filtered it through his clothes to reduce the salt
He left it in the sun to evaporate the salt

question: Fill in the blank: ________ I told you a secret, would you be able to keep it? (uncertain possibility)
Choose the correct option: 'I wish I were taller.' expresses ________.
choices: a past regret about something that happened
a complaint about someone else's behaviour
a present wish or desire about something you cannot change now
a future plan that might happen

question: Fill in the blank: I ________ forward to hearing from you. (formal letter closing phrase)
